name: Moroccan Lentil Soup
recipeIngredient: 15 ounces garbanzo beans, canned and drained
recipeInstructions:
1. In a soup pot, sauté onions in 2 tablespoons of broth until they are softened.
2. In a blender or food processor, combine tomatoes with their juice, spices, and cilantro. Puree until fairly smooth.
3. Add the purée to the onions. Simmer covered for 5 minutes.
4. Add lentils, wheat berries, and broth or water. Cover tightly. If using a slow cooker, set on medium heat for 8-10 hours or low heat overnight. On the stove, simmer until the wheat is tender, about 1-1/2 hours.
5. About 15 minutes before serving, add garbanzo beans.
6. About 10 minutes before serving, beat egg with juice of one lemon. Reduce heat to simmer; stir egg mixture into soup until strands form.
7. Season with salt and pepper. Serve by squeezing the juice of the remaining lemon into soup bowls before ladling in the soup.
